housing.summary: Students are required to live in dorms for the first two years, after which most move to nearby off-campus apartments. North campus offers traditional dorm living while south campus offers a different experience. Shaw Hall (Architecture LLC) has pod bathrooms and a dining hall in-building but is considered less social.
key_quotes: You're required to for the first two years. But you can for all four. A lot of ppl move off campus after the first two into apts nearby and walk to campus.
Shaw is the dorm that's closest to studio...heated sidewalks for the walk to campus on a snowy day
Don't do Shaw its considered the worst dorm socially unless ur vibe is more of the less social ppl

social_life.summary: Syracuse has a strong Greek life and party culture, with sororities sometimes having their own chefs. The social scene is vibrant but heavily tied to Greek life, which may not suit everyone. Roommate boundary issues (like uninvited guests staying for months) do appear to be a real concern.
key_quotes: If you live on South or join Greek life, some sororities have their own chef
How do I tell my roommate that I don't want to share the room with his brothers and cousins? They've been here for months!
incredible school spirit, great academics

dining.summary: Dining is standard cafeteria fare with multiple halls of varying quality. Students recommend BBB and Graham as the better options, though they require a longer walk. DoorDash is popular but gets expensive quickly.
key_quotes: It's gonna be cafeteria food. Basically what you've been eating the past 14 years of your life in school. Just with more choices.
If you aren't super busy then BBB and Graham are worth the walk.
Doordash gets expensive, dont rely on that.

mental_health.summary: There is very limited direct discussion of mental health resources at Syracuse in these posts. The general college mental health themes (stress, anxiety, difficulty eating) appear but without specific mention of SU's counseling services or support systems.
key_quotes: I've been struggling to eat in College...I've been really stressed about getting all my assignments submitted on time
I'm a little out of it
My grades suck, I couldn't focus on anything, I've been the most exhausted I've ever been in my life

financial_aid.summary: Financial aid appears negotiable at Syracuse, with students reporting success getting additional funding by meeting with regional representatives or explaining personal circumstances. However, aid is not infinite and the process requires proactive outreach.
key_quotes: I got an extra 5k just by meeting with my the lady in charge of my region
I got an additional 8k after telling them about my circumstances. Have you tried that?
I just turned down my spot due to tuition.

academics.summary: Syracuse is mentioned positively for specific programs including journalism (direct admit), architecture, Maxwell School, and communications. The architecture program is notoriously demanding with students sleeping in studios. Internship opportunities for freshmen in tech are limited but improve by junior year.
key_quotes: Getting an internship in CE/CS as a freshman is extremely uncommon especially at a big company.

Joining an LLC lets you immediately connect with other people in your major and a faculty member which may be helpful later on down the road when looking for internships and letters of recommendation.
Syracuse University (Direct admit to Journalism)

administration.summary: Limited direct feedback on administration, but the financial aid office appears responsive to students who proactively reach out. The RA system has some reported issues, with one RA allegedly being absent most weekends.
key_quotes: Your RA needs to be fired
My RA's parents pick her up at 3:45am every Friday and drop her off again on Sunday.
financial aid is not infinite

location_and_campus.summary: Syracuse is described as having one of the most gorgeous facilities among safety-tier schools. The campus has heated sidewalks for snowy winters, which is a practical perk. The surrounding city of Syracuse is considered fairly mid by locals but offers some amenities.
key_quotes: Syracuse University (most gorgeous facilities)
heated sidewalks for the walk to campus on a snowy day
living in a city that is... pretty mid

career_outcomes.summary: Career outcomes vary significantly by major. Journalism and communications graduates benefit from a strong program reputation, while CS/engineering students face a competitive market. Most students land meaningful internships between sophomore/junior and junior/senior years.
key_quotes: Most students land internships in the summer between sophomore/junior year and the summer between junior/senior year.
There are many, many opportunities for computer engineering majors in many industries
In terms of investment I would choose Fordham, especially if your not a big partier and not into the Greek scene. And Fordham being so close to NYC it is very good for finance majors

value_for_money.summary: Syracuse is expensive and some students turn down admission due to tuition costs. However, merit aid and negotiated financial aid packages can significantly reduce costs. The school is frequently mentioned as a high-quality safety school offering good value when scholarships are factored in.
key_quotes: I just turned down my spot due to tuition.
I got an additional 8k after telling them about my circumstances.
high quality safety schools with top programs include Syracuse University

overall_vibe.summary: Syracuse has strong school spirit, a beautiful campus, and passionate students who love their Orange identity. It is a classic big university experience with Greek life, sports, and a tight-knit major community. Students who engage with the school tend to thrive, but those looking for a quieter or more urban experience may find it limiting.
key_quotes: incredible school spirit, great academics
i'm a freshman at syracuse and absolutely love it!
I'm excited to attend

red_flags.summary: The most concrete red flag is a reported RA who was essentially absent every weekend, suggesting inconsistent residential life oversight. Roommate boundary violations (guests living in the room for months) also point to enforcement gaps. Additionally, one comment noted Syracuse attracted similar caliber students to Boston University 30 years ago but has since diverged, hinting at prestige concerns.
key_quotes: My RA's parents pick her up at 3:45am every Friday and drop her off again on Sunday. Your RA needs to be fired
How do I tell my roommate that I don't want to share the room with his brothers and cousins? They've been here for months!
30 years ago, they attracted similar caliber of students [to BU]. That's one university that has taken a different direction.

hidden_gems.summary: The Architecture LLC in Shaw Hall offers heated sidewalks, an in-building dining hall, and immediate connections to faculty that can pay off for internships and recommendations. Financial aid is more negotiable than most students realize — a direct meeting or written appeal can yield thousands in additional aid. The Maxwell School gives politically-minded students access to faculty like Chandra Mohanty who are genuinely engaged with prospective students.
key_quotes: heated sidewalks for the walk to campus on a snowy day
I got an extra 5k just by meeting with my the lady in charge of my region
very interested in chandra mohantys gender studies work and have already corresponded with her
